name: Smothered Steak
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the steak and cut into serving-size pieces.
2. Pound each piece with a meat mallet or the edge of a saucer to tenderize.
3. In a bowl, combine flour, salt, and pepper. Dredge each piece of meat in the flour mixture.
4. Pound the seasoned meat again using the mallet or plate edge.
5. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Brown each piece of steak in batches until browned on all sides.
6. Remove the steaks from the skillet and set aside.
7. Add more oil to the skillet if needed, then add sliced onions, cooking until softened.
8. Lower the heat to medium and return the meat pieces with onions to the skillet.
9. Add water and cover the skillet. Cook over medium heat until the steak is tender and the gravy has thickened, about 30-45 minutes.
10. If the gravy becomes too thin, remove the lid for the last 15 minutes of cooking or stir a bit of flour into the pan to help thicken it.
